metric_value: For the past three months, a 2.0-GHz Pentium 4 microprocessor has been considered the standard for the corporate mainstream desktop, representing the best value in performance and price for the vast majority of enterprise workers.

metric_name: 2.4 GHz tier added at same price
metric_value: Dell, HP, and IBM recently added the 2.4-GHz Pentium 4 microprocessor to their single-price lower Pentium 4 price tier — corporate IT managers can order a 1.8-, 2.0-, 2.2-, or 2.4-GHz Pentium 4 desktop and pay the exact same price at checkout.
Continuing the trend toward faster, smaller, and cheaper.

Intel clock-speed race stalled at ~3.8 GHz due to thermal limits; Pentium 4 was supplanted by multi-core Core microarchitecture starting 2006; corporate standard shifted to clock-for-clock and core count rather than clock speed.
